verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2) is a secreted serine protease produced predominantly by prostate epithelium, released into seminal fluid and serum. It has a signal peptide and is constitutively secreted with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or documented wash-resistant covalent tethering to a surface partner. While it can transiently bind protease inhibitors (e.g., PCI) in solution, this is reversible equilibrium, not stable surface anchoring. Clinical interest (e.g., radioligand/ADC programs) targets the secreted/tissue-bound pool, not a bona fide surface pool.
